questions.explanation: From option (d), 30 ÷ 5 + 4 * 10 - 15 = 6 + 40 - 15 = 46 - 15 = 31 Hence, option D is the correct answer.

questions.text: Suppose in a certain language MADRAS is coded as NBESBT. Then BOMBAY is coded in that language as:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: To solve this problem, we need to identify the pattern used in the given code. Each letter in the original word is replaced with the next letter in the English alphabet, and the resulting string is shifted one position to the right. For example, M → N (shifted one position to the right) A → B (shifted one position to the right) D → E (shifted one position to the right) R → S (shifted one position to the right) A → B (shifted one position to the right) S → T (shifted one position to the right) Using this pattern, we can encode BOMBAY as: B → C (shifted one position to the right) O → P (shifted one position to the right) M → N (shifted one position to the right) B → C (shifted one position to the right) A → B (shifted one position to the right) Y → Z (shifted one position to the right) Therefore, the correct answer is (a) CPNCBZ.

questions.explanation: As we can see the code exchanges each letter with the letter that follows it, so A becomes B, B becomes C, C becomes D, and so on. We will do this for each letter in Bombay and will arrive to: CPNCBZ.

questions.explanation: All Options excluding C refers to animals and objects that are involved in flying. Squirrel is the only option that is present on the ground.

questions.text: UDOMETER is coded as DUMOTERE then how will SUBLEASE be coded?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The correct answer is (d) USLBEAES. The coding pattern used here is that each group of three letters is reversed and then the first and last letters of the resulting group are interchanged. Applying this pattern to "UDOMETER", we get "DUMOTERE" as given. Applying the same pattern to "SUBLEASE", we get: "SUB" reversed becomes "BUS" "LEA" reversed becomes "AEL" "SE" reversed becomes "ES" So the coded word is "BUSAELES". Interchanging the first and last letters of each group, we get "USLBEAES". Therefore, the correct answer is (d).

questions.explanation: The way this is coded is that alternate letters in the word are moved one word forward. So U went after D, O went after M, E went after T, and the second last E goes after R. Hence for SUBLEASE, S will go after U, B will go after L, E will go after A, and S will go after E, giving us USLBAEES.

questions.text: Identify which do not possess the same kind of meaning as the others:
questions.subject: English
questions.explanation: Honesty and Integrity mean the same thing which is speaking the truth and obeying ethics and morals. Risk and Danger also mean the same thing which is something being perilous and hazardous. Pain and Agony also mean the same thing which is something that hurts a lot. Bondage and Freedom do not mean the same thing, freedom means being free and having escaped while bondage means being held in captivity, prison.

questions.text: A man walks 3 km northwards and then turns left and goes 2 km. He again turns left and goes on 3 km. He turns right and walks straight. In which direction he is walking?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: The man initially walked 3 km northwards, then turned left and walked 2km westwards. Then he turned left and walked 3 km southwards. Finally, he turned right and walked straight. This means he is walking towards the west direction. Therefore, the answer is (b) West.

questions.explanation: When the man was walking northwards, he was on the path of North. When he turned left he started walking towards the West. When he turned left again he started walking towards the South. Turning right from here, puts him back on the West pathway.

questions.text: In the following question, a statement is given followed by four inferences. You have to decide which of the given Inferences can definitely be drawn from the given statement. Indicate your answer. Statement: Most dresses in that shop are expensive. Inferences: I. Some dresses in that shop are expensive. II. There are cheap dresses also in that shop. III. Handloom dresses in that shop are cheap. IV. There are no cheap dresses available in that shop.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Answer: b) Only 2 follows Explanation: The statement says that "most dresses in the shop are expensive". This means that there could be some dresses in the shop that are not expensive. Therefore, inference 1) "Some dresses in that shop are expensive" is definitely true. Inference 2) "There are cheap dresses also in that shop" is also true as the statement does not say that all dresses in the shop are expensive. However, inference 3) "Handloom dresses in that shop are cheap" and inference 4) "There are no cheap dresses available in that shop" are not supported by the given statement and hence cannot be drawn.

questions.explanation: According to given statement, most dresses are expensive so there are some cheap dresses also in that shop.

questions.explanation: There are four babies i.e Bilal, Jamila, Javed and Junaid. In increasing order of weight, the babies are Javed, Jamila and Junaid but we cannot say that Javed weighs the heaviest since we do not know the weight of Bilal.

questions.text: During the past year, Jamshed saw more movies than Sadia.Sadia saw fewer movies than Dawood.Dawood saw more movies than Jamshed.If the first two statements are true, the third statement is
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Incorrect
Incorrect
Explanation given below
Incorrect

questions.explanation: Statement 1 = Jamshed > Sadia (True) Statement 2 = Sadia < Dawood or Dawood > Sadia (True) Statement 3 = Dawood > Jamshed (Uncertain) Since Statement 1 and 2 both imply that Sadia has seen less number of movies than, both Dawood and Jamshed, there is uncertainty about who has seen more movies between Jamshed and Dawood as not enough information is given. It can either be Dawood > Jamshed > Sadia OR Jamshed > Dawood > Sadia .

questions.text: Assume that floors are polished on consecutive instead of nonconsecutive days but that all other scheduling policies are unchanged. For how many of the six days can it be determined both whether plants are watered and whether floors are polished?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Given that floors are polished on Monday, based on the question's assumptions, floors must also be polished on Tuesday and Wednesday — i.e., on three consecutive days. Since plants are not watered on consecutive days (that policy is unchanged), they must be watered on Thursday and Saturday. Neither task is performed on Friday. The correct response is D.

Given that floors are polished on Monday, based on the question's assumptions, floors must also be polished on Tuesday and Wednesday — i.e., on three consecutive days. Since plants are not watered on consecutive days (that policy is unchanged), they must be watered on Thursday and Saturday. Neither task is performed on Friday. The correct response is D.

Given that floors are polished on Monday, based on the question's assumptions, floors must also be polished on Tuesday and Wednesday — i.e., on three consecutive days. Since plants are not watered on consecutive days (that policy is unchanged), they must be watered on Thursday and Saturday. Neither task is performed on Friday. The correct response is D.

Given that floors are polished on Monday, based on the question's assumptions, floors must also be polished on Tuesday and Wednesday — i.e., on three consecutive days. Since plants are not watered on consecutive days (that policy is unchanged), they must be watered on Thursday and Saturday. Neither task is performed on Friday. The correct response is D.

questions.explanation: Given that floors are polished on Monday, based on the question's assumptions, floors must also be polished on Tuesday and Wednesday — i.e., on three consecutive days. Since plants are not watered on consecutive days (that policy is unchanged), they must be watered on Thursday and Saturday. Neither task is performed on Friday. The correct response is D.

questions.text: Statements: - A > B, B ≥ C, C < D Conclusions (I) A > C (II) A = C
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Let's analyze the given statements and conclusions: Statements: 1. A > B 2. B ≥ C 3. C < D Conclusions: (I) A > C (II) A = C Let's evaluate each conclusion: Conclusion (I): A > C From statement 2, we know that B ≥ C. Combining this with conclusion (I) that A > C, we cannot definitively determine the relationship between A and C. It is possible that A is greater than C, but we cannot establish this based on the given information. Conclusion (II): A = C There is no direct information in the statements to support conclusion (II) that A = C. Statement 2 tells us that B ≥ C, but it does not give any direct relationship between A and C. To summarize, based on the given statements, we cannot confirm either conclusion (I) or conclusion (II). The relationships between A, B, and C remain ambiguous. It is essential to have more information or additional statements to draw definitive conclusions about the relationships between these variables.

options.text: Neither I nor II is true

questions.explanation: if A is greater than equal to B and B is greater than C then A is greater than C. If A is greater than C, it can not be equal to C.

questions.text: A book cannot exist without?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: If there is no education, books still exist. Hence, this option is incorrect.
If pictures do not exist, the book still may exist. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Theoretically speaking, if there are no pages, there may be no book, because a book is made up of pages. Hence, this option is the correct one.
If there is no qualification, the books will remain unaffected and may still exist. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: A book can still exist if it doesn’t have pictures; it can be a reading book without pictures; it might be a book to read for leisure and not for education; and it might not have any qualifications, but it will still be a book. However, without pages, a book cannot physically exist.

questions.text: What should come next to infirmary?

questions.explanation: An infirmary is a place that takes care of the infirm, sick, or injured.Without patients, there is no infirmary. Surgery (choice a) may not be required for patients. So the only option left is (choice c) C so it is correct.

questions.text: Verbal Reasoning: Country with the highest muslim population is _.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Pakistan is the second most populous Muslim-majority country in the world. It is located in South Asia and has a population of over 220 million people, of which about 96% are Muslim. Islam arrived in Pakistan in the 8th century and has since become the national religion.

Malaysia is the third most populous Muslim-majority country in Southeast Asia. It has a population of over 32 million people, of which about 60% are Muslim. Islam arrived in Malaysia in the 14th century and has since become the official religion.

Indonesia has the highest Muslim population in the world. It is estimated that around 87% of Indonesia's population identifies as Muslim, making it the country with the largest Muslim population globally. Therefore, option C, Indonesia, is the correct answer.
Iran is a country in Western Asia. It has a population of over 80 million people, of which about 90% are Muslim. Islam arrived in Iran in the 7th century and has since become the official religion.

questions.explanation: Indonesia is the most populous Muslim-majority country in the world. It is located in Southeast Asia and has a population of over 270 million people, of which about 87.2% are Muslim. Islam arrived in Indonesia in the 14th century and has since become the dominant religion.

questions.text: Verbal Classification. Frame : Picture
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option presents a relationship between a person (a criminal) and an action or concept (a crime). However, this relationship is not a specific type of association. Criminals are typically associated with crimes, but this relationship is not unique or defining. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

This option presents a clear relationship between two objects. A nail is a tool typically used with a hammer. The hammer is used to drive the nail into a surface. This relationship between a tool and its corresponding object is a specific and defining association. Therefore, this option is correct.

This option presents a relationship between two natural elements. While rivers and forests can exist together in certain landscapes, their relationship is not one of dependence or direct association. Rivers can flow through forests, but they can also flow through other types of terrain. Therefore, this option is incorrect.

This option presents a relationship between two components of a whole. Binding is a process or material used to hold the pages of a book together. The binding is an integral part of a book, and without it, the book would not be complete. This relationship is a specific and defining association between two elements of a whole. Therefore, this option is correct.

questions.explanation: A frame surrounds a picture. In a similar way, a binding surrounds the book.

questions.text: Statements and Conclusions:All fish are tortoises. No tortoise is a crocodile.Conclusions: I. No crocodile is a fishII. No fish is a crocodile
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: a) Only conclusion II followsConclusion II states, "No fish is a crocodile." This conclusion follows directly from the given statements. If all fish are tortoises (Statement 1) and no tortoise is a crocodile (Statement 2), then it logically follows that no fish can be a crocodile. So, conclusion II is correct.
b) Either I or II followsThis option is not correct. Both conclusions are not contradictory, and they can both be true simultaneously. So, it is not a case of either/or.
c) Neither I nor II followsThis option is not correct. Conclusion II ("No fish is a crocodile") follows from the given statements.

questions.text: Analogies:Choose a word that is a necessary part of education system:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: While meetings might be held in an educational context, they are not a necessary part of the education system itself. Meetings can be conducted for administrative purposes or to discuss matters related to education, but they are not essential components of the system.
Sports and physical activities are often encouraged and beneficial in the education system for the overall development of students, but they are not necessary in every educational setting.

Students are the core and essential part of the education system. Without students, there would be no educational process or system. The primary purpose of the education system is to educate and impart knowledge to students.
Tests or examinations are an integral part of the education system. They are used to assess students' understanding, knowledge, and progress in their studies.

questions.explanation: The term education system generally refers to schooling. For schooling, the only necessity is students to teach. Tests and extracurriculars like sports may or may not take place. Meetings to decide what to and what not to teach are all optional. But if there are no students then the education system can not survive.

questions.text: Analogies:Marathon is to race as Hibernation is to
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: a) WinterExplanation: A marathon is a specific type of race that involves running a long distance, usually 26.2 miles (42.195 kilometers). The relationship between marathon and race is that a marathon is a specific type of race.

b) BearExplanation: This option does not establish a clear analogy between hibernation and bear. While it is true that some bears hibernate during the winter, hibernation is not exclusive to bears. Other animals also hibernate during the winter. The analogy should focus on the behavior of hibernation rather than a specific animal.

c) DreamExplanation: This option does not establish a clear analogy between hibernation and dream. Hibernation is a biological state of dormancy and inactivity that some animals experience during the winter to survive harsh conditions. Dreams are mental experiences that occur during sleep and are not directly related to hibernation.

d) SleepExplanation: This option establishes a clear analogy between hibernation and sleep. Hibernation is a specific behavior exhibited by some animals during the winter to conserve energy and survive harsh conditions. Sleep is a state of rest and reduced activity that all animals, including humans, experience regularly. The relationship between hibernation and sleep is that hibernation is a specific type of prolonged sleep exhibited by certain animals.

questions.explanation: 'Marathon' can be regarded as an extensive and extended form of 'race'. Similarly, 'hibernation' is an a extended form of 'sleep'.

questions.text: Directions: Read the following information and give answer.A, B. C, D and E are five friends. B is elder to E, but not as tall as C. C is younger to A. and is taller to D and E. A is taller to D, but younger than E. D is elder to A but is shortest in the group.Which of the following pairs of students is elder to D?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Let's analyze the information step by step:1. B is elder to E.2. C is younger to A, but taller than D and E.3. A is taller than D, but younger than E.4. D is elder to A, but shortest in the group.From the above information, we can deduce the following:- E is elder to B, A, C, and D (since B is elder to E, but E is not elder to B, it means E is actually younger than B is not true, but E is elder to A, C and D)- A is younger than E, but elder to C (since C is younger to A)- C is younger to A, but taller than D and E- D is elder to A, but shortest in the groupNow, let's find the pairs of students elder to D:- E is elder to D (directly mentioned)- B is elder to E, which means B is also elder to D (transitive relation)- A is younger than E, but we don't know A's relation with B directly, however A is younger than E and E is younger than B, hence B is elder to A and A is elder to D doesn't make A elder to D but B is elder to D via E and also via A indirectly- C is younger to A, and A is younger to E, and E is younger to B, hence C is younger to B, and B is elder to DTherefore, the pairs of students elder to D are:- E and BHowever, since the question asks for "pairs," the correct answer would be the combination of two individuals. Considering this, we can say:- B and EAnswer: B and E.

questions.explanation: Given Information: - A, B, C, D and E are five friends. - B is elder to E, but not as tall as C. - C is younger to A, and is taller to D and E. - A is taller to D, but younger than E. - D is elder to A but is shortest in the group. To find: Which of the following pairs of students is elder to D? Solution: Let's try to summarize the given information and create a table to better understand the relations between the friends: Friend | Age | Height --- | --- | --- A | Younger than D and taller than E | Taller than D B | Elder than E and shorter than C | --- C | Younger than A and taller than D and E | Taller than D and E D | Elder than A and shortest in the group | Shortest in the group E | Elder than A and shorter than C | --- From the table, we can see that: - B is elder to E, but not to D. - C is younger to A, and is taller to D and E, but not elder to D. - A is younger than E, but taller than D, and not elder to D. - D is elder to A, but shortest in the group, so no one is elder to D.Read more at:

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain.A fruit basket contains more apples than lemons.There are more lemons in the basket than there are oranges.The basket contains more apples than oranges.If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: There are fewer oranges than either apples or lemons, so the statement is true.Easy method: (Try this method to solve without any confusion)1. A fruit basket contains more apples than lemons = App > Lem2. There are more lemons in the basket than there are oranges = Lem > OrgNow, Combine the above two results: App > Lem > Org3. The basket contains more apples than oranges (App > ... > Org) = Yes.Therefore, the given 3rd statement is true.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Mara runs faster than Gail. Lily runs faster than Mara. Gail runs faster than Lily. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: We know from the first two statements that Lily runs fastest. Therefore, the third statement must be false.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Joe is younger than Kathy. Mark was born after Joe. Kathy is older than Mark. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: Joe is younger than Kathy and older than Mark, so Mark must be younger than Kathy.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Oat cereal has more fiber than corn cereal but less fiber than bran cereal. Corn cereal has more fiber than rice cereal but less fiber than wheat cereal. Of the three kinds of cereal, rice cereal has the least amount of fiber. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: From the first statement, we know that bran cereal has more fiber than both oat cereal and corn cereal. From the second statement, we know that rice cereal has less fiber than both corn and wheat cereals. Therefore, rice cereal has the least amount of fiber.

questions.text: Each problem consists of three statements. Based on the first two statements, the third statement may be true, false, or uncertain. Battery X lasts longer than Battery Y. Battery Y doesn't last as long as Battery Z. Battery Z lasts longer than Battery X. If the first two statements are true, the third statement is:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.explanation: The first two statements indicate that Battery Y lasts the least amount of time, but it cannot be determined if Battery Z lasts longer than Battery X.
